§09.label: MOST DANGEROUS — locative claims about non-existent files

blockers.claim: compass shipped the Effect Stream-Hub-PubSub primitives. these are the arteries for activityStream and populationStore
blockers.truth: compass uses hand-rolled Set<callback> subscribe pattern · zero matches for Hub/PubSub/Stream in lib/runtime/, lib/activity/, lib/sim/ · only weather + sonifier are on Effect Layers
blockers.fix: rewrite §08 §1 to honest current state: 'compass shipped Effect Layers for weather + sonifier · the activityStream + populationStore still use hand-rolled subscribe(cb), flagged by construct-effect-substrate SKILL.md as the next adoption target'

blockers.claim: Solana parallel column · Anchor emit! events · program logs
blockers.truth: loa stack has no Solana layer · ecosystem-architecture.md L1-L5 are framework/protocol/runtime/platform/product · compass mints Metaplex NFTs but ships no Anchor program · §08 conflicts with §09's ERC-6551 EVM column
blockers.fix: either delete the Solana column from §07 (downgrade to 'optional anchor target') OR commit to it and remove §09's ERC-6551 framing

blockers.claim: the architectural anchor is the ERC-6551 token-bound account · puruhani as the spine
blockers.truth: no 6551 implementation exists in loa-* / freeside-* / compass · puruhani in compass is a sprite/CDN asset (lib/world-purupuru-cdn.ts:100-117) · vault doctrine puruhani-as-spine names 'mint-on-demand, never mint-at-onboarding'
blockers.fix: downgrade to 'when on-chain materialization is needed, ERC-6551 is the canonical pattern (see puruhani-as-spine doctrine)' · do not assert it as shipped

blockers.claim: every envelope must carry signature or substrate-truth pointer · these four are non-negotiable across all altitudes
blockers.truth: no schema, no codec, no example · in-memory subscribe(cb) events have no chain anchor and no signature · the rule is unimplementable as written for code-altitude
blockers.fix: adopt IMP-3's Effect Schema definition · narrow 'signature' to discriminated union of {ed25519 sig, substrate-pointer with txSig+slot} so code-altitude has a valid encoding

blockers.claim: this split is enforced at the construct-boundary.port.ts interface
blockers.truth: file doesn't exist in compass, construct-effect-substrate, or loa · invented in §09 with passive voice ('is enforced at') · a claim of enforcement requires a typed interface and a Layer; neither shipped
blockers.fix: ship the file. IMP-4 provides the paste-ready interface that fences verify ⊥ judge at compile time
blockers.evidence_path: no path · file does not exist

high_consensus.title: Ship the actual Effect Schema for the event envelope
high_consensus.current: prose-only spec: '[id · trace · scope · payload · signature]'
high_consensus.proposed: import { Schema as S } from 'effect'; export const EventEnvelope = S.Struct({ id: S.UUID, trace: S.Struct({ parent: S.NullOr(S.UUID), root: S.UUID, emittedAt: S.DateFromString }), scope: S.TemplateLiteral(...), provenance: S.Struct({ emitter: S.String, altitude: S.Literal('substrate', 'runtime', 'distribution') }), payload: S.Unknown, signature: S.Union(S.Struct({ kind: S.Literal('ed25519'), sig: S.String }), S.Struct({ kind: S.Literal('substrate-pointer'), txSig: S.String, slot: S.Number })) }); export type EventEnvelope = S.Schema.Type<typeof EventEnvelope>
high_consensus.rationale: skeptic B4 + judge cross-check + improver IMP-3 all converge: the envelope must be a Schema, not prose · without it §09 §10 §11 all reference 'the envelope' as if shipped

high_consensus.title: Ship construct-boundary.port.ts as a real typed interface
high_consensus.current: passive-voice claim 'this split is enforced at construct-boundary.port.ts'
high_consensus.rationale: skeptic B5 + judge identifies §09 as MOST DANGEROUS + improver IMP-4 ships the interface · turns 'verify ⊥ judge' from code-review hope into compile-time fence

high_consensus.title: Mark daemon-NFT-as-composed-runtime locative claims as PROPOSED
high_consensus.current: the stack axis is defined in daemon.schema.ts. the civic alignment is enforced by governance.port.ts. the exodia composition is assembled in exodia.live.ts. time is recorded via the event envelope in memory.system.ts.
high_consensus.proposed: rewrite as 'PROPOSED layout: stack → daemon.schema.ts (proposed) · civic → governance.port.ts (proposed) · exodia → exodia.live.ts (proposed) · time → memory.system.ts (proposed) · community → pubsub channels'. Add a §Files-to-build section so Sprint 1 can execute the proposal.
high_consensus.rationale: all 4 named files are invented · judge: 'pure invention dressed as map' · skeptic: 'phantom files' · improver implicitly addresses by demanding real signatures

high_consensus.title: Add typed error channel to mintTBA Effect.gen pseudocode
high_consensus.current: Effect.gen function with mintTBA, initializeState, connectEventStream, resolveVoice — no signatures, no errors, no requirements
high_consensus.proposed: Mark as illustrative AND retype as Effect.Effect<Daemon, MintFailure | SchemaDrift | StreamUnavailable | VoiceResolutionError, TBAClient | MetadataStore | EventBus | FinnRuntime>. Annotate each yield* with what it can fail with.
high_consensus.rationale: skeptic missing-deps + judge weakest-claim + improver IMP-5 · the operator's whole point is typed-error envelopes (§08); §09's canonical lifecycle should embody it

high_consensus.title: Add Test substrate row + Identity/owner column to translation table
high_consensus.proposed: 4 rows (Substrate / Runtime / Distribution / Test) × 5 columns (the 4 + Identity/owner). Test row: '*.mock.ts Layer + property tests | hounfour invariant suite + finn sandbox replay | shadow-fork TBA + simulated metadata mutations | solana-test-validator + Anchor bankrun | CI / fagan reviewer'
high_consensus.rationale: improver IMP-1 + IMP-2 · the four-folder pattern explicitly names *.mock.ts (per construct-effect-substrate patterns/suffix-as-type); leaving the test row out hides the symmetry that makes the doctrine FAGAN-safe; identity column turns table from descriptive to actionable

high_consensus.title: Replace aspirational promotion criteria with measurable gates
high_consensus.current: adopted by ≥3 distinct projects + route events between 2 different constructs + test counter-examples
high_consensus.proposed: Quantitative: ≥1 envelope round-trip (substrate→runtime→distribution→back) measured under 200ms p95 in a non-Next.js project. Counter-example test must include: malformed sig · replay (same id twice) · scope-mismatch routing. ≥1 promotion project must be Solana (the §07 column is otherwise aspirational).
high_consensus.rationale: judge weakest-claim + improver IMP-10 · 'adopted by 3 projects' is the same gate as construct-effect-substrate (still candidate); without sharper gates the translation pack will sit candidate forever too

disputed.title: Puppet theater — vapor or productive thesis?
disputed.skeptic_view: no three.js code in compass · 'experimentation thesis' is promoting an unbuilt artifact · §10 violates suffix discipline (puppet.component.ts not in pack)
disputed.improver_view: the 3-altitude event visibility is the proof-of-isomorphism · IMP-7 adds Mermaid + IMP-8 expands to schema + mock + test files
disputed.resolution_proposal: Adopt IMP-7 Mermaid diagram NOW (concrete) · downgrade puppet theater prose to 'proposed thesis · MVP file list to ship in cycle N+2' · add the Mermaid above 'the puppet theater is not a demo' paragraph

disputed.title: Are finn / hounfour 'constructs' or 'layers'?
disputed.skeptic_view: loa/docs/ecosystem-architecture.md:55-59 puts finn at L3 Runtime and hounfour at L2 Protocol · they are LAYERS, not constructs · constructs are a cross-cutting distribution plane
disputed.improver_view: (does not address — IMP-6 just expands composes-with frontmatter)
disputed.resolution_proposal: Rewrite §07 §08 §09 to use 'finn (the runtime)' and 'hounfour (the protocol/schema layer)' · reserve 'construct' for the cross-cutting plane (observer, crucible, artisan, etc.) per ecosystem-architecture's 'Construct Lifecycle' section

naming_drift.issue: invented; not a contract path or TS convention
naming_drift.fix: improver IMP-11 proposes 'loa-hounfour repo at programs/daemon-relay/ (Anchor program) with TS port at loa-finn/src/relay/relay.port.ts'

naming_drift.issue: *.component.ts is not in the substrate pack suffix discipline (only port/live/mock/system)
naming_drift.fix: rename to puppet.entity.ts OR add component to the pack's suffix doctrine

naming_drift.issue: finn is the runtime (L3), not a construct
hounfour is the protocol/schema repo (L2), not a construct
BEAUVOIR.md is the bridgebuilder/soul template; reusing the name conflates two artifacts
naming_drift.fix: qualify as 'BEAUVOIR.md voice file (per dAMP-as-default-voice-substrate doctrine, distinct from Loa bridgebuilder template)'

naming_drift.issue: never defined anywhere; appears to be invented atmosphere
naming_drift.fix: delete reference OR cite the actual ceremony commit (compass commit 41a4aaa style(ceremony): Hades pattern)

steel_man.claim: ECS≡Effect≡Hexagonal isomorphism as canonical mental model
steel_man.preserves: operator's discovered truth from compass cycle · removing forces 3 vocabularies to be re-translated at every boundary
THE artifact · load-bearing translation device · survives any rewrite

steel_man.claim: Substrate verifies, construct judges (with: 'never route on-chain value through LLM verdicts')
steel_man.preserves: safety invariant · without it mibera-as-NPC collapses into 'LLM controls assets'
operationalizes SM-1 at daemon scale · Effect.gen example is paste-ready scaffold

steel_man.claim: Event envelope carries provenance + scope + idempotency + signature/substrate-truth pointer (§08)
steel_man.preserves: cross-altitude wire format · removing means each integration re-invents replay protection
what makes puppet theater a doctrine artifact, not a demo · same envelope projected onto 3 surfaces IS the proof of isomorphism

steel_man.claim: Metadata document as sync forcing function (§11 emergence check)
steel_man.preserves: names the hidden glue · already validated in continuous-metadata-as-daemon-substrate (4-layer daemon stack)

recommendation.rationale: 5 BLOCKERS · §09 scored 1630/4000 (MOST DANGEROUS) · Solana vs ERC-6551 chain confusion across §07/§08/§09 is structurally incoherent · 4 of 5 files reference the envelope schema as if shipped when it doesn't exist
recommendation.next_action: Address all 5 BLOCKERS + 6 HIGH_CONSENSUS items + the 7 naming drifts. Re-review the corrected files individually (avoiding the long-prompt PROVIDER_DISCONNECT). Consider whether the operator wants to feed the synthesis report back to Gemini for a v2 of the 5 docs.
